artifacts.description: Execution log documenting dataset retrieval attempt, environment constraint, and reason no empirical results were produced.

limitations: No accessible analysis file containing the required variables was available in the container during this run.
The hinted BRFSS GitHub mirror appears to be a diabetes-indicators subset, not a full BRFSS extract with e-cigarette and epilepsy fields.
Python network retrieval from raw.githubusercontent.com failed in this environment due to name-resolution/network access issues.
Without a valid dataset containing age, sex, e-cigarette use, and epilepsy variables, meaningful original empirical results were not possible.
methods: I evaluated the user’s idea against the datasets hinted in this run and attempted a reproducible retrieval of the BRFSS 2015 diabetes health indicators CSV from the specified GitHub raw mirror. I also inspected web-search results to verify what the mirrored BRFSS-derived dataset contains. In this run, Python-based data retrieval from raw.githubusercontent.com failed because outbound network resolution was unavailable in the execution environment, and there was no local copy of the dataset under /mnt/data. The web-inspected GitHub materials indicated that the available BRFSS-derived mirror is a diabetes-focused indicator subset rather than a full BRFSS file, so it is not an adequate primary dataset for estimating e-cigarette prevalence or testing an epilepsy association among women under 50. Because no valid analysis dataset containing both e-cigarette exposure and epilepsy outcome variables was accessible in this run, I did not fabricate data or run unsupported statistics. I saved a run log documenting the failed retrieval and feasibility constraint.
research_question: Among women younger than 50 years, what is the prevalence of e-cigarette use, and is e-cigarette use associated with epilepsy?

sources.label: GitHub mirror project describing the BRFSS-derived diabetes indicators dataset
sources.notes: Used to verify that the accessible mirrored BRFSS-derived dataset is diabetes-focused and based on a reduced feature set, not obviously suitable for e-cigarette/epilepsy analysis.

sources.label: CDC BRFSS 2015 annual survey data page
sources.notes: Used as contextual provenance that full BRFSS 2015 exists separately from the reduced GitHub diabetes-indicators mirror.

title: Feasibility assessment for e-cigarette prevalence and epilepsy in women under age 50
